answer:
We are given  
\[
P(x)=\frac{(x^{2310}-1)^6}{(x^{105}-1)(x^{70}-1)(x^{42}-1)(x^{30}-1)}.
\]  
Since \(2310 = \text{lcm}(105,70,42,30)\), each denominator factor divides \((x^{2310}-1)\). Using the factorization  
\[
x^n-1 = \prod_{d\mid n} \Phi_d(x),
\]  
the expression simplifies to a product of cyclotomic polynomials. The exponent of each \(\Phi_d(x)\) in the numerator is \(6\) times the number of times \(d\) divides \(2310\), and in the denominator it is the sum over the four numbers of whether \(d\) divides them.

6 \cdot \mathbf{1}_{d\mid 2310} - \bigl
